rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Maimaran and Wheeler (2008) as a specific prior finding that variety seeking can be nonconsciously influenced by shape arrays. It then positions its own visual priming approach as adding a novel way to induce or reduce variety seeking, relative to that prior work. Even though the citation is brief and appears mainly in the contribution discussion, it serves a clear positioning function beyond a generic literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends prior evidence from Maimaran and Wheeler (2008) on nonconscious visual induction of variety seeking by proposing a different visual manipulation and linking induced variety-seeking versus inertia to immediate versus delayed promotion preferences.
